prompt: Wide architectural view of a dated 1990s Methuen MA kitchen BEFORE remodel. Builder-grade golden oak raised-panel cabinets with brass-tone teardrop pulls. White four-inch ceramic tile counter with discolored grout. A single fluorescent ceiling fixture with a yellowed plastic diffuser provides flat overhead light. Cracked sheet vinyl floor in a faux-tile pattern. An almond-colored side-by-side fridge to the left, a dingy stove on the right. Shot with a 24mm wide architectural lens, harsh top-down fluorescent light dominates, slight green color cast left honest. A red Milwaukee tool case set on the counter as the only fully saturated color, ready for tear-out. High-contrast slightly desaturated, working-contractor proof-of-work feel, NOT HGTV bright, NOT staged. No readable text, no logos.
